mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate directly reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, compressing required returns and driving positive S&P 500 returns. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over 60 days signals monetary easing that mechanically increases present value of future corporate cash flows.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 3.63% provides room for rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative equity returns. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, equity risk premium expands and SPX declines.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.3% is near historical lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer demand strength and forward earnings visibility. When RSAFS decelerates below its 6-month moving average, it signals weakening consumer spending that reduces near-term corporate revenue growth, producing negative 5-day returns as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth falls below 6-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of deceleration-triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) captures real economic capacity utilization and supply-side momentum. Sustained INDPRO growth above 1% month-over-month signals robust supply expansion and productivity gains that support corporate margins and equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of high-growth INDPRO instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate simultaneously (both above 3-month moving averages), it indicates broad-based demand strength that drives positive equity returns over 20 days through improved earnings visibility and multiple expansion.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both above respective 3-month moving averages
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1465.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 757085.0 million dollars
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of dual-acceleration instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) jointly determine real wage dynamics and labor market slack. When PAYEMS growth decelerates while CPIAUCSL accelerates, it signals stagflationary pressure that compresses real incomes and equity valuations, producing negative 20-day returns as investors reprice terminal growth rates downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159001 thousand persons, CPIAUCSL at 333.979
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of stagflation-signal instances, or interaction term is not significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 4.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated (month-over-month growth > 0.3%), the Fed is in restrictive tightening mode that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced earnings growth. This regime produces negative 60-day returns as monetary headwinds persist.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary tightening with persistent inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of restrictive-regime instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic demand and future corporate earnings capacity. When GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, it signals robust demand that supports equity valuations and produces positive 60-day returns. This mechanism is always-on and operates across all macro regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 24152.656 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of high-growth GDPC1 periods, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
